The 150th episode of the rural medical drama, starring Eva Pope and Simon Shepherd

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: As Alex and Tom have cause to celebrate, Will tries to sort out his tangled personal life and deals with the perplexing case of a depressed gardener found weeping by a pond. Popular medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Victor McGuire and Susannah Corbett

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch discovers fellow inmates at Slade Prison are planning an escape over Christmas - and is so convinced the bid is doomed to fail that he takes refuge in the hospital so he cannot be blamed when the tunnel is found. Festive edition of the classic comedy from 1975, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch plans to bring a bit of Christmas spirit to Slade Prison in the form of his own home-made booze. But when Mackay and Barrowclough uncover the stash in his and Godber's cell, the crafty con seems in danger of spending the yuletide period in solitary confinement. Festive edition of the classic comedy from 1976, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A diabetic girl disappears without her insulin, and Lynley discovers she was last seen talking to a couple who had previously been sighted with a youngster later found drowned in a lake. The detective stages a press appeal, hoping to learn more about the mysterious pair and reveal their roles in both cases. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: Spooks

However, Harry and Lucas are suspicious and, after launching a secret surveillance operation on the magnate's mansion, they discover he is planning an attack using a deadly nerve agent, with potentially disastrous consequences. Starring Sophia Myles and Peter Firth

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Margo insists that Jerry keep fit so he will be well enough to enjoy his retirement when it comes. Tom realises he and Barbara have no money set aside for their old age and presents a bank manager with an unusual proposition. George Cole guest stars in the final episode of the sitcom, made for the 1978 Royal Command Performance

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Margo returns her entire Christmas order because the tree is too short - and she and Jerry are left with no means to celebrate after the department store fails to redeliver in time. Tom and Barbara come to the rescue, inviting them round for a Christmas of homemade paper hats and improvised party games. Seasonal special from 1977, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Keith, Paul Eddington and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: Victoria Wood's Midlife Christmas
programme.desc: A festive special from 2009 that attempts to cram an entire night's Christmas viewing into 60 minutes. Highlights include the Midlife Olympics 2009, popular costume drama Lark Pies to Cranchesterford and the adventures of soap star Bo Beaumont, played by Victoria's long-time collaborator Julie Walters. Others joining in the fun are Delia Smith, Jayne Torvill, Christopher Dean, Reece Shearsmith and Anton Du Beke

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After recruitment for his Merry Men hits an all-time low, a letter from an enthusiast with excellent credentials gets Billy excited again, and he organises a committee to greet the newest member. Long-running comedy, starring Keith Clifford and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: With Bessomer Street's car crime statistics on the up, the pressure mounts for Maisie as she organises an operation to infiltrate a highly professional ring of automobile thieves, while simultaneously coping with a serious mistake made by an officer going through a personal crisis. Crime drama, starring Pauline Quirke and Richard Graham

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: When a priceless violin is stolen, the staff of the Drake Detective Agency set out to track down the culprit. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith as the first female private detective in 1920s Toronto

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 10-year-old murder of a librarian who also belonged to a witches' coven, and tries to track down the other members of the group. Halford has visions of his dead wife, and becomes convinced he has been cursed, while Lane starts to suspect the killing may have been part of a human sacrifice. With guest star Stephanie Beacham

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Learning that the Azakstan Freedom Front is trying to acquire a lethal nerve agent, Harry sends Lucas to help the Russians destroy the sample. However, a member of the AFF travels to London in search of the substance, forcing the team to work quickly and welcome an FSB officer to the grid in a bid to track down the rogue nationalist and prevent the use of a biological weapon that has the potential to wipe out the entire city in hours. Guest starring Julian Lewis Jones (Where the Heart Is)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The annual St Peter's Fair gets under way in Shrewsbury but a gruesome murder dampens the festivities. Antagonism between local merchants and visiting traders becomes increasingly ugly, and as civil unrest rages in the streets, Beringar is ordered to kill his friend Cadfael, whose investigations are causing King Stephen acute political embarrassment. Medieval whodunit, starring Derek Jacobi, Eoin McCarthy and Michael Culver

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing monk investigates the murder of a pregnant woman and the priest who rejected her confession. At first it looks as though suicide is a major factor in the case, but Cadfael is not convinced and, of course, he is proved right once the clues start falling into place. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.desc: Having finally recovered from the death of his beloved Teresa, Sharpe marries Jane Gibbons. Before the honeymoon is over, he is forced to rush off and capture a fort behind French lines - and while he is away a fever outbreak threatens to cut short his new marriage. Sean Bean stars

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A serial arsonist targets sites related to the 1983 general election. After risking his life in a burning building following a quiet word from Keats, Ray gets personally involved in the high-profile investigation - which forces him to face his traumatic past as it becomes clear the tragic events of the Falklands War are driving the firestarter to murder. Fantasy drama, with Dean Andrews and Joe Absolom (Doc Martin)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene jeopardises a covert operation into a drug cartel when he learns several dealers have been murdered recently and the crimes are linked to an old adversary. Chris falls for the attractive undercover officer, but his feelings prompt a violent outburst that threatens his future in the police, and Alex gathers more clues about why she remains in the 1980s. With guest star Zoe Telford (Criminal Justice)

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: An unidentified woman is killed outside an air force base commanded by Phryne's old flame Lyle Compton, leading to tensions between the military and the Melbourne police

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective uncovers a tale of forbidden romance as he investigates the death of a man from Toronto's Jewish ghetto. Ogden concludes the victim, Nathan Siebold, died of as a result of a consistent and lengthy exposure to arsenic, and though still alive the object of his affections, Devra Bergleman, is also showing symptoms of the toxin. She adored Nathan, though apparently had been held away for him in preference to another suitor. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective investigates the savage murder of a prominent banker and his two large dogs, apparently carried out by a wild beast. programme.desc: The detective and sidekick Jardine investigate when a prominent loan shark dies after being brutally attacked with an axe. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki ignites a war and Jack delivers some unwanted news. As Tina's trust in Gray wavers, she seeks out answers

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain continues her culinary journey with a visit to California, home to one of the most diverse populations in America. She is invited to a Mexican quinceanera - an extravagant fiesta celebrating a girl's 15th birthday - cooks for an all-woman daredevil rodeo riding team and makes handmade fortune cookies with a grandma in one of the last remaining workshops in San Francisco's Chinatown

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2020 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge. They are given three hours and 15 minutes to cook a starter, a main course and a dessert, each of which takes their culinary abilities to a whole new level, with flawless presentation and exciting flavours. Judges Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Gregg Wallace assess their efforts, before deciding the winner

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund and Lord Melchett are embarrassingly kidnapped by a maniacal German master of disguise with designs on the English throne. Unfortunately for them, the Queen decides to pay only one of the ransom demands. Comedy, guest starring Hugh Laurie, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Fortitude star Robert Sheehan appears as himself in this festive episode of the sitcom. The residents of the council estate where Conor and Jock live are threatened with eviction, but are not about to go down without a fight - and the resulting media circus puts everyone on edge. In the midst of all this mayhem, Conor's mother Mairead is rushed to hospital, and may be more seriously ill than previously thought. When she says she wants to see a white Christmas, the lads vow to make her wish come true - with a little help from Sheehan, who is shooting a film nearby

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Lichfield, Staffordshire, Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent and Sutton in Surrey

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: There's a huge boost to the breeding programme, as an anteater gives birth in the run-up to Christmas. As Kate Humble reviews the footage with the keepers it reveals some worrying behavior from the mum towards her newborn. their stable at night in the winter months. Meanwhile, Ben Fogle contemplates whether reindeer really fly and Jean Johansson gives the lions their Christmas stockings and continues to find out if any animals really like sprouts.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross trawls through the programme's archives to select the best gifts bought by the teams from Christmases past and shares some trade secrets on what to look out for when seeking great festive presents. Plus, Anita Manning learns about the first Christmas cards and visits celebrity cook Rosemary Shrager to try out some festive treats with a difference

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple want to spend their retirement in the peace and quiet of the Scottish borders and Alistair Appleton is on hand to help them find the perfect pad for their £350,000 budget. Alistair also visits a mill that has been weaving the country's iconic fabric, tweed, since the early 19th century

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne searches for three items he can rescue and revamp at the recycling centre in Woking. An old plans chest needs some careful reworking at the hands of restorers Tom and Johnny, while woodworking wizard Norman Wilkinson is challenged with creating high-end furniture from a set of discarded garden chairs. EJ himself attempts to repurpose a pair of swivel stools

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: This south-west adventure concludes with a tour of Devon, Somerset and Dorset for auctioneers Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon. Izzie visits a top secret location and makes a discovery, while Southon buys some Georgian shoe buckles and an anchor. Izzie also plans an upset at the final auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones introduces the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by cyclist Rachel Atherton, cricketer Ollie Pope, six-time world swimming champion Mark Foster and former footballer Clinton Morrison. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie reaches its peak as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda makes a decision from which there is no coming back. Kheerat tries to do the right thing and is surprised at what he finds. It's time to cast the role of Walford's bad Santa - and with Kim taking the reins, things quickly descend into chaos

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs for a compilation that looks back at the big news of 2020

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Grandad has got it into his head that the house is haunted, Rory hopes to improve his looks by undergoing plastic surgery and Cathy continues her search for the right man by getting herself an internet date. As for Agnes, her annual battles with the decorations may well be over as Buster has bought her a special tree. What could possibly go wrong? Brendan O'Carroll stars as the daft-but-doting Irish matriarch

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to George Clooney about his new sci-fi movie The Midnight Sky. Michael Sheen and David Tennant discuss playing locked-down actors in the second series of Staged, while Oscar-winner Viola Davis talks about the drama Ma Rainey's Black Bottom. Vanessa Kirby reflects on domestic drama Pieces of a Woman, Daisy May Cooper discusses her latest work, and Michael Ball and Alfie Boe perform Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The family members finish their big Christmas Day dinner and Barbara senses that her turkey wasn't up to scratch. Soon after Antony leaves for girlfriend Emma's house and Dave departs to take Nana home, Denise goes into labour upstairs in the bathroom and Jim tries to comfort her. Festive episode of the comedy from 1999

programme.title: What We Did on Our Holiday (2014)
programme.desc: A couple whose marriage has hit a rocky patch take a trip to Scotland with their three children, to visit the husband's father on his 75th birthday. They try to conceal their marital strife to avoid spoiling the occasion, but have to contend with the kids giving away all their secrets. Comedy drama, starring Rosamund Pike, David Tennant and Billy Connolly

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Angela Hartnett, and comedian Micky Flanagan. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Everton v Chelsea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall looking for two things to save from the clutches of the tip crusher. With the help of artisan Norman Wilkinson, Sarah hopes to turn a profit

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair in tonight's episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Comedian Omid Djalili, reality star Charlotte Crosby, TV presenter Dr Zoe Williams and former England rugby international James Haskell answer questions on the Harry Potter Films, Curb Your Enthusiasm, Nelson Mandela, and Whales, Dolphins and Porpoises

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie and Maisie perform a dance chosen by the judges, their showdance and their favourite routine from the series. The people with the paddles - Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse - will critique each performance, but it will be up to the viewers alone to determine who will be taking the glitterball from reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher. There'll also be a performance by pop superstar Robbie Williams

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize, guided by tonight's seven celebrities, who include Chris Kamara, Gyles Brandreth and Carol Vorderman

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present the latest action, including the Thursday night match between Las Vegas Raiders and Los Angeles Chargers

programme.title: Notes on a Scandal (2006)
programme.desc: Lonely history teacher Barbara Covett's acerbic personality means she has few friends. When a spirited woman takes a job in the art department of the school where Barbara works, the elderly lady develops a dark obsession with her new colleague, and the discovery of an affair with a student sets her on a mission to destroy the life of her new 'friend'. Drama, starring Judi Dench, Cate Blanchett, Bill Nighy, Tom Georgeson and Michael Maloney

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: The eland herd is growing fast after the introduction of a young male, while Jean Johansson discovers she helped kick-start the biggest flock of newborn flamingos in years

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon search for antiques on the Cornish coast, where Izzie picks up some Scandinavian jewellery, and Catherine finds a Bristol Blue glass lamp stand

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of first-time puppy owners. Rescue dog Elina moves in with the Orchard family in Buckinghamshire after a 2,000-mile trip from Cyprus, where she was found abandoned on the side of the road. Meanwhile, Sage the Staffordshire bull terrier cross is a massive hit with the Fianko family

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Victoria, Yasmin and Jordon must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of TV interviews with actor David Niven, examining the appearances on chat shows he made over the years. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: The Bishop's Wife (1947)
programme.desc: Distraught bishop Henry is struggling to cope with building a new cathedral, but receives help from on high in the form of a suave guardian angel. The heavenly visitor proves influential in fundraising, but when he turns his attention to Henry's family life, the cleric suspects his new friend has designs on his wife. Fantasy, starring Cary Grant, David Niven and Loretta Young

programme.title: The Polar Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan and the team return to Svalbard in mid-June on the trail of Lyra and her two cubs to find out how they are coping with the unusual Arctic weather. Gordon locates the family and renews his bond with the adult bear, but discovers that cub Luca is missing and documents the struggle to survive as the mother bear tries to keep her remaining offspring safe

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Caroline Hawley head to the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall, where they unearth items including an unusual tortoise and an electric guitar

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the finalists' friends and family, who share their thoughts on how their loved ones will do, and designer Vicky Gill appears one last time to reveal some of Saturday night's costumes

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory that produces two million tins of festive chocolate assortments a year, with 5,500 individual sweets packaged and dropped into containers every minute. Cherry Healey learns how the Royal Mail produces Christmas stamps, and also travels to Germany to join a crew of 35 ornament decorators applying glitter and paint to glass Santas. Historian Ruth Goodman goes on the trail of the festive turkey, a tradition that began in the 16th century when these birds were first introduced from Mexico

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Linus the Bedlington terrier joins the Seymour family, but mum Rebecca is not sure she has made the right decision. Cookie the French bulldog moves in with hairdresser Louise, personal trainer Phil and their two young boys. The family have high hopes she will fit in with their active lifestyle, but are thrown when she refuses to go out on walks. In n West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo has now been with retirees Linda and Andrew for a month and shows no signs of slowing down

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: The final documentary looks at one of the greatest challenges faced by modern Elizabethans - the loss of Britain's manufacturing heartlands and the surprising impact it has had on the state of the nation. Andrew Marr selects a rich cast of characters, from Christopher Cockerell to Clive Sinclair, who in his opinion have responded to the challenges and found ingenious ways of adapting to the changing industrial landscape

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig has a Christmas knees-up in the pub with Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Josh Widdicombe and Noel Fielding. Hostelry-themed questions are the order of the day, with a sprinkling of festive cheer along the way

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Everyone Romesh has ever met has told him how amazing the Scottish islands are - but nobody he knows has actually ever been there. Now, in a year when travel abroad has been all but forbidden, he is heading to the Hebrides to find out if you can really have a proper adventure so close to home, spending a week visiting the isles of Skye, Harris and Lewis. Guiding him through the journey will be Donald Macsween, or Sweeney to his friends, a local crofter from the far north of Lewis - a place that lies as far north as Greenland. During their trip, Sweeney will show Romesh the spectacular rugged beauty of the islands and what at first glance look like Caribbean sandy beaches (but turn out to be utterly freezing). Rom will try his hand at the Highlands Games and try to avoid feeling up a sheep's testicles during a day spent on Sweeney's farm. Along the way, there will be a trip to the remote island of St Kilda - four hours out across the open Atlantic - and a chaotic afternoon spent with lo

programme.title: An Education (2009)
programme.desc: A hard-working, intelligent 16-year-old is on her way to gaining a place at Oxford University. However, her conventional education is called into question when an older, worldly suitor takes her under his wing and exposes her to some of the finer things in life. Coming-of-age drama set in the 1960s and based on Lynn Barber's memoir, with Carey Mulligan, Peter Sarsgaard, Alfred Molina, Cara Seymour and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Russell Kane and Tracy-Ann Oberman to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including the revival of Doctor Who, and Paula Radcliffe winning the London Marathon.

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver oversees proceedings as previous winners of the culinary contest prepare a feast for the heroes of the pandemic - beginning with a course of canapes. In a twist on previous series, the eight cooks will be split into two teams of four and will judge the other group's efforts in a blind taste test. Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen will be joining the teams in the kitchen

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the canape and starter heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. With Andi Oliver hosting proceedings, there is a vacancy in the judging chamber, so joining Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton is actor and comedian Kerry Godliman

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: The lions need a new scratching post and Jean Johansson finds out exactly what happens at a meerkat Christmas party

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon's search takes them to Devon, where items include a 1920s flapper dress, violins, trinket boxes and a walking stick with a maritime connection

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, four experts pool their talents to tackle the repair of a battered old ventriloquist's dummy, before silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a silver running trophy that survived a direct hit during the Blitz in the Second World War. Elsewhere, the experts turn their attention to a vintage portable typewriter and an old shove ha'penny board

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create an Austrian linzertorte, roast goose and all the trimmings, and a hazelnut chocolate brownie with clementine sorbet, as they find out how guest Aled Jones spends his Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun, there are also fantastic ideas for home-made edible Christmas presents

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel the country to examine the UK's obsession with teatime treats. They make Bakewell tart in its original Derbyshire home and prepare a Victoria sandwich for the ladies of the WI. At Henley regatta, the duo prepare cookies with sour cherries and chocolate chips, and later serve afternoon tea to the Tees Rowing Club

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: The Polar Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: When Gordon Buchanan and the team return to Svalbard in September, they face a big challenge to find mother and cub as the tracking collar hasn't sent a signal for a month. They do eventually locate them and discover Lyra is very thin and has been reduced to eating moss, seaweed and even plastic fishing floats to stave off hunger. The bear's milk is also starting to run dry and she is refusing to let Miki suckle - while her desperation for food means that for the first time she sees Gordon as a possible meal

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series and bringing brand new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin takes a journey down the River Tweed and uncovers the history of the unusual border town, Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal presents all the backstage gossip from Strictly Come Dancing, as the remaining contenders prepare for tomorrow night's grand final

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: Food writer Nigella Lawson shares her foolproof guide to stress-free festive cooking and entertaining, featuring a selection of celebratory dishes that are both indulgent and practical. She prepares a slow-cooked black treacle ham to replace the traditional turkey as the party centrepiece, paired with a potato and pepper bake and a sweet and sour vegetable slaw. She also makes a trio of cakes, beginning with a delicately flavoured cider and five-spice sponge topped with salted caramel sauce, followed by a lemon and thyme version, and ending with a pumpkin bundt adorned with an aromatic orange icing

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: The rains hit the waterhole, and for the first time in half a year, grass begins to grow and water is everywhere, dramatically changing the fortunes of the wild residents. The waterhole area is totally transformed into a lush grazing pasture and a pair of Egyptian geese take up permanent residence on the waterhole's edge. Using remote cameras, Chris Packham reveals how the waterhole hyena clan copes with these new circumstances, while Ella Al-Shamahi uncovers the huge shift in the waterhole timetable and spots new competition flooding into the area - wildebeest

programme.title: The Happy Prince (2018)
programme.desc: Biographical drama about the final years of Oscar Wilde. After his release from Reading prison for gross indecency, the once celebrated and now notorious playwright lives out his final years in self-imposed exile in France. He is visited by loyal friends and lovers, but none seems able to divert him from resuming the destructive relationship with Alfred 'Bosie' Douglas. Written by directed by, and starring Rupert Everett, with Colin Firth, Tom Wilkinson, Anna Chancellor, Colin Morgan and Emily Watson

programme.title: Corin Redgrave on Oscar Wilde's De Profundis
programme.desc: The actor recites an extract from the playwright's letter to his lover Lord Alfred Douglas, written in Reading jail

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam presents analysis of the day's national and international news stories

programme.title: The Importance of Being Oscar
programme.desc: A star-studded documentary drama providing an account of Oscar Wilde's glittering career before his trial for homosexual crimes and his tragic fall from grace. Wilde enthusiasts and experts such Stephen Fry and Wilde's grandson Merlin Holland provide revelatory accounts of how the writer's own life informed his work, intercut with adaptations of his comedies and books performed by a cast including Freddie Fox, Claire Skinner, Anna Chancellor and James Fleet

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: When an embittered civilian takes key military and government officials hostage aboard Cloud Nine, Starbuck launches a rescue attempt which will have tragic consequences for one of the crew. Sci-fi drama, with Jamie Bamber and Katee Sackhoff

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Tensions run high between Lee and the new commander of the Pegasus when a Raptor team goes missing and he leads the ship into great peril. The arrival on Galactica of a pregnant girl seeking to terminate her unborn child prompts a political struggle when Baltar diasgrees with Roslin's decision to criminalise abortion, and announces he is running for the presidency

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past 11 months, the BBC's Medical Editor Fergus Walsh has followed the team at Oxford University and AstraZeneca as they designed, developed, manufactured and trialled their Covid-19 vaccine. In this programme he charts their progress, as well as scrutinisng the trial data and examining the vaccination's efficacy and safety

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: Documentary following the rigid, class-obsessed Britain of the 1950s to the more liberal, inclusive society in the latter part of the Queen's reign. Andrew Marr begins by taking in the liberal victories of the 'permissive society' - as well as the ferocious backlash of middle England at the perceived erosion of family values. He also looks at people's attitudes to movie starlet Diana Dors and wannabe movie starlet Ruth Ellis - who achieved fame of a completely different kind

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including an alien-like snail with pulsating antennae and a 10-metre long animal called a pyrosome

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his band of travellers visit a panda reserve in China, learn how kulfi ice-cream is made in India, and pay tribute to the Black Eyed Peas in Luang Prabang

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joshua, 11, and his younger brother Owen are great friends, but Owen has Downs syndrome, which means that a lot of his parents' time is taken up looking after him. Joshua loves DJ'ing and admires Radio 1's Annie Mac. Here, helpers make Joshua his very own DJ den

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: It's our big Christmas special! Expect a studio full of festive fun with a music mash up from 220 Kid and JC Stewart, a huge Christmas medley featuring virtual choirs all over the UK; plus cards and messages from our BP fans. And Mwaksy and Richie take to the sky in a glider to put on a huge firework display

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Natural history film comparing the fortunes of polar bears with grizzlies by following two families in the wild. As the Arctic warms up, the creatures' habitats are beginning to overlap, causing the animals to compete for food and space, and in some cases interbreed. Narrated by Peter Guinness

programme.title: The Greatest Story Ever Told (1965)
programme.desc: The life of Jesus, from His lowly birth in Bethlehem and the miracles He performed, to His ultimate betrayal at the hands of disciple Judas Iscariot and His death by crucifixion. Biblical drama, starring Max von Sydow, David McCallum, Telly Savalas, Charlton Heston, Angela Lansbury and Sidney Poitier, plus several cameos including John Wayne

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Cookbook
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers plan a festive get-together for the people they have met during their TV adventures - but while promising plenty of food and drink, they decide there will be no turkey and trimmings, focusing instead on the best party food they can prepare. So begins a UK-wide search for canapes, mini-desserts, Thai treats and Indian delights, before they build the ultimate centrepiece for the party - a vodka ice luge - and turn their venue into a winter wonderland ready for their guests to arrive

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days (1956)
programme.desc: Victorian gentleman Phileas Fogg wagers he and his loyal valet Passepartout can circumnavigate the globe in only 80 days. Oscar-winning period adventure, based on Jules Verne's novel, starring David Niven, Cantinflas and Shirley MacLaine, with cameo appearances by stars including Noel Coward, Marlene Dietrich, Frank Sinatra, Buster Keaton and John Gielgud

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: David Harewood narrates a documentary exploring three of the most exotic and remote islands on the planet, beginning with the unique and extraordinary wildlife of Madagascar. As the oldest island on Earth, life has had time to evolve and there are now more unique plants and animals on Madagascar than any other, with footage of ring-tailed lemurs, labord's chameleons and Decken's sifakas

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared at the end of today's programme

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch plans to bring a bit of Christmas spirit to Slade Prison in the form of his own home-made booze. But when Mackay and Barrowclough uncover the stash in his and Godber's cell, the crafty con seems in danger of spending the yuletide period in solitary confinement. Festive edition of the classic comedy from 1976, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: The Eagle Has Landed (1976)
programme.desc: A Nazi secret agent plans to kidnap Winston Churchill while the prime minister spends a weekend in the Norfolk countryside, and plants covert operatives in the area ready for his arrival. Second World War adventure, adapted from Jack Higgins' novel, starring Michael Caine, Donald Sutherland, Robert Duvall, Jenny Agutter, Donald Pleasence, Anthony Quayle and Jean Marsh

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The actor reminisces about his movie career, which has spanned more than 60 years, in a series of archive interviews woven together with contributions from colleagues and critics, plus clips from some of his most famous films. They include Zulu, which gave the future knight his first starring role, espionage adventure The Ipcress File, gangster thriller Get Carter and the 2009 film Harry Brown, in which he appeared as a vigilante OAP

programme.title: My Generation (2017)
programme.desc: Michael Caine narrates and stars in his own personal journey through 1960s London. Based on personal accounts and archive footage, this documentary sees Caine travel back through time to talk to the Beatles, Twiggy, David Bailey, Mary Quant, the Rolling Stones, David Hockney and other star names. The film has been assembled over the last six years by Caine working with producer Simon Fuller, writers Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais, and director David Batty, to tell the story of the birth of pop culture in London through the eyes of the young Michael Caine

programme.title: Educating Rita (1983)
programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters

programme.title: Great Expectations (2012)
programme.desc: A penniless orphan is chosen by a reclusive spinster to be a playmate for her cold-hearted ward, whom he falls in love with. As he grows up, a mysterious benefactor offers him the chance to rise into high society, but dark secrets lie behind both the object of his adoration and his newfound fortune. Period drama based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring Jeremy Irvine, Helena Bonham Carter, Ralph Fiennes and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the second semi-final of the academic quiz's festive series. Jeremy Paxman hosts

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross uses striking blue tones to produce a night-time winter scene, complete with his trademark cabin, inside an oval surround

programme.title: Far from the Madding Crowd (2015)
programme.desc: A headstrong Victorian woman inherits a sheep farm in a remote area of Dorset. She struggles to maintain her independence while being courted by three very different men - a stoic farmer, a dashing, reckless soldier and a lonely older man. Period drama based on Thomas Hardy's novel, starring Carey Mulligan, Matthias Schoenaerts, Tom Sturridge and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A tribute to the renowned London-born pop diva, featuring BBC archive footage of some of Dusty's most famous performances from 1961 to 1995. The programme also offers a chance to relive the singer's story, from her folk beginnings with the Springfields, through to her Motown homage years and her collaboration with Pet Shop Boys in the late 1980s. Also includes the star's duets with Tom Jones and Mel Torme

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Pop diva Dusty Springfield hosts the final edition of her 1960s entertainment showcase, chatting to Scott Walker of the Walker Brothers, and performing her chart-topping 1966 single You Don't Have to Say You Love Me. Originally broadcast in 1967

programme.title: Tamara Drewe (2010)
programme.desc: A woman returns to the sleepy village where she grew up, having transformed from an awkward teenager to a successful and confident journalist. She instantly becomes the object of obsession for every man in the neighbourhood, while her sex life becomes a source of fascination for two local girls. Comedy based on Posy Simmonds' comic strip, with Gemma Arterton, Dominic Cooper and Tamsin Greig

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross uses striking blue tones to produce a night-time winter scene, complete with his trademark cabin, inside an oval surround

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Roper discovers that crucial documents have been leaked and becomes suspicious that he has a traitor in his team. Pine is asked to demonstrate weapons to one of the arms dealer's clients, and is confronted with the full horror of Roper's actions. Dromgoole tries to persuade Burr to abandon her investigation, leaving her afraid for her family's safety

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Roper's final deal with Barghati takes place in Cairo, and Pine is forced to return to the Nefertiti Hotel, where he first became aware of the arms dealer. This leads him to unexpectedly bump into the owner, jeopardising his cover story. Burr faces the consequences of her failed mission to intercept one of Roper's arms shipments, and suspects Pine has turned against her. Jed risks her life to obtain the combination to a safe containing vital documents - but has to do so under the watchful gaze of Caroline

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as teams from Wadham College, Oxford, and the University of Leeds - each made up of four distinguished graduates - compete in the 2019 final of the festive quiz

programme.title: Cigar Box Blues - The Makers of a Revolution
programme.desc: Documentary about the creators and players of hand-made guitars, which have a long history in American blues music but have only recently become popular in the UK. The film meets the fervent advocates of the 'cigar box guitar revolution', such as blues guitarist and singer Seasick Steve, and shows how the democratic, pro-recycling, local production ethos of the movement inspires new recruits, and that the emotional connection they feel for their instruments creates a unique and evocative sound

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on May 24, with performances by artists including Beats International, En Vogue, Mantronix featuring Wondress, Michael Bolton, Don Pablo's Animals, Was (Not Was) and the B-52s

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on May 31, with performances by artists including Black Box, Talk Talk, Sam Brown, Erasure, Chad Jackson, England and New Order, the Charlatans and Adamski

programme.title: The Sound of TV with Neil Brand
programme.desc: Neil reveals how television scores have grown in importance, from their origins in the 1960s and 1970s to reach a peak in the big-budget world of Netflix and HBO. He demonstrates how music has driven the success of BBC natural history programmes, talking to George Fenton, the film composer behind such landmarks as Blue Planet. Fenton was also pivotal in the development of music in TV drama, with his score for Jewel in the Crown. Plus, maverick creators of the TV score, including Roxy Music's Andy MacKay, who wrote the songs that powered radical '70s drama Rock Follies and David Chase, music buff and creator of The Sopranos

programme.title: The Story of A Fairytale of New York
programme.desc: Richard E Grant narrates a look at the making of the Pogues and Kirsty MacColl's classic bittersweet love song. The group return to the studio where the song was recorded, while director Peter Dougherty and actor Matt Dillon discuss the making of the video - which resulted in a brush with the law. With contributions from Jools Holland, Nick Cave and producer Steve Lillywhite

programme.title: Country Christmas
programme.desc: Festive performances by the biggest names in country music, including Dan + Shay, Darius Rucker and Lindsey Stirling, Florida Georgia Line, Gabby Barrett, Kelsea Ballerini, Lady A, Little Big Town, Tim McGraw and host Thomas Rhett

programme.title: Carole King and Friends at Christmas
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs festive favourites at a concert that also features Sheffield crooner Richard Hawley, retro vocal trio the Puppini Sisters, Brighton group the Mummers, and New York jazz vocalist Gregory Porter. Among the songs on the bill are Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, Silent Night, Let It Snow, White Christmas and The Christmas Song

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Pet Shop Boys and Enya feature among the top hits and videos from 1988, while Cliff Richard sings Mistletoe and Wine, that year's Christmas Number one. Introduced by Bruno Brookes, Gary Davies and Anthea Turner

programme.title: The Story of A Fairytale of New York
programme.desc: Richard E Grant narrates a look at the making of the Pogues and Kirsty MacColl's classic bittersweet love song. programme.title: Cigar Box Blues - The Makers of a Revolution
programme.desc: Documentary about the creators and players of hand-made guitars, which have a long history in American blues music but have only recently become popular in the UK. The film meets the fervent advocates of the 'cigar box guitar revolution', such as blues guitarist and singer Seasick Steve, and shows how the democratic, pro-recycling, local production ethos of the movement inspires new recruits, and that the emotional connection they feel for their instruments creates a unique and evocative sound

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Another chance to see Ronnie Barker's final screen appearance, originally aired two months after his death in 2005, in which he and Ronnie Corbett choose their favourite sketches, songs and monologues from their festive shows, including the Milkman's Christmas Message, Christmas Day in the Yukon and an interpretation of Alice Through the Looking Glass. Katie Melua sings Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, before the duo sign off for the very last time

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bjork and Jack Dee introduce the biggest-selling songs of 1995, plus the Christmas Day Number One - Michael Jackson's Earth Song. Featuring performances by N-Trance, Annie Lennox, Take That, Boyzone, the Outhere Brothers, Simply Red, Robson and Jerome, Pulp and Blur

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent embarks on a trip back in time by setting the remote control for December 1995 and serving an irreverent look back at the festive viewing options that faced the nation

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Christmas special from 1995. The Meldrews' wedding anniversary celebrations turn sour as they learn cousin Ursula has died while watching Noel Edmonds - forcing Victor to defy a witch's grim warning and set out to put her affairs in order

programme.title: Saturday Night Fever - The Ultimate Disco Movie
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the 40th anniversary of the blockbuster dance movie. Bruno Tonioli, a young dancer in New York in 1977, revisit the streets where the film was shot and looks back at the success of a production that gave everyone 'disco fever'. Tonioli and contributors including its star John Travolta, recount how a small-budget film and a quickly assembled soundtrack became a mega-hit that changed popular culture for ever. The programme also hears about the potent influence of impresario Robert Stigwood, whose faith in the young Travolta and then-stuttering pop group the Bee Gees proved visionary

programme.title: Bee Gees at the BBC and Beyond
programme.desc: A compilation of classic performances by the legendary pop trio from the BBC archive. Including renditions of the brothers' most popular hits, 1960s appearances on European television performing I Started a Joke, footage from a Top of the Pops staging of World, and some later concerts from the 1990s

programme.title: Disco at the BBC

programme.desc: Arthur Sleep hosts a Christmas quiz featuring funny festive clips and a seasonal singalong. Professor Muddles and Ann Teak join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt as they attempt to win not just Gigglepoints but some special Christmas Gigglepresents too. The first round is a simple clip round with a question for each team. The second round kicks off with Nana Knickerbocker’s Panto Showdown, featuring Nana’s cows in a Christmassy Kitchen attempting to make messy Christmas puddings. Then it's on to the third and final buzzer round, and this is Gail and Keith’s last chance to win their Gigglepresents. They both get ready on their buzzers as Arthur reveals a special previously unseen festive clip, featuring DIY Dan clumsily trying to decorate a Christmas tree. Professor Muddles has a bright idea to end the show on a singalong. And so, the teams gather round to sing us out with a lovely rendition of We Wish You a Giggle Quizmas.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Fortitude star Robert Sheehan appears as himself in this festive episode of the sitcom. The residents of the council estate where Conor and Jock live are threatened with eviction, but are not about to go down without a fight - and the resulting media circus puts everyone on edge. In the midst of all this mayhem, Conor's mother Mairead is rushed to hospital, and may be more seriously ill than previously thought. When she says she wants to see a white Christmas, the lads vow to make her wish come true - with a little help from Sheehan, who is shooting a film nearby

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two divers documenting a wreck in the Thames find more than they bargained for and call in the Royal Navy bomb team. An unsafe load flies onto the windscreen of another driver

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: When flooding hits a business, the owner goes to extraordinary lengths to try to rubber stamp her claim. A dog owner displays very bad behaviour when it comes to their pet's insurance policy, while there's a backlash over a whiplash case when footage emerges of the claimant in very fine health indeed. Narrated by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: There's a huge boost to the breeding programme, as an anteater gives birth in the run-up to Christmas. As Kate Humble reviews the footage with the keepers it reveals some worrying behavior from the mum towards her newborn. their stable at night in the winter months. Meanwhile, Ben Fogle contemplates whether reindeer really fly and Jean Johansson gives the lions their Christmas stockings and continues to find out if any animals really like sprouts.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross trawls through the programme's archives to select the best gifts bought by the teams from Christmases past and shares some trade secrets on what to look out for when seeking great festive presents. Plus, Anita Manning learns about the first Christmas cards and visits celebrity cook Rosemary Shrager to try out some festive treats with a difference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda makes a decision from which there is no coming back. Kheerat tries to do the right thing and is surprised at what he finds. It's time to cast the role of Walford's bad Santa - and with Kim taking the reins, things quickly descend into chaos

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs for a compilation that looks back at the big news of 2020

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Despite being fully aware of his limited knowledge of all things botanical, the comedian becomes a florist in Porthcawl. Cutting his teeth during the crazy Valentine's rush, Rhod has days to master this creative skill, before the business competes in the heats of the Chelsea Flower Show

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to George Clooney about his new sci-fi movie The Midnight Sky. Michael Sheen and David Tennant discuss playing locked-down actors in the second series of Staged, while Oscar-winner Viola Davis talks about the drama Ma Rainey's Black Bottom. Vanessa Kirby reflects on domestic drama Pieces of a Woman, Daisy May Cooper discusses her latest work, and Michael Ball and Alfie Boe perform Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce looks for a way to better cope with her anxiety, attending a yoga class with a difference and discovering the benefits of performing random acts of kindness to strangers. She also seeks the advice of actress-turned-blogger Holly Matthews, who has learned to find peace following a terrible tragedy in her life

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Grandad has got it into his head that the house is haunted, Rory hopes to improve his looks by undergoing plastic surgery and Cathy continues her search for the right man by getting herself an internet date. As for Agnes, her annual battles with the decorations may well be over as Buster has bought her a special tree. What could possibly go wrong? Brendan O'Carroll stars as the daft-but-doting Irish matriarch

programme.title: What We Did on Our Holiday (2014)
programme.desc: A couple whose marriage has hit a rocky patch take a trip to Scotland with their three children, to visit the husband's father on his 75th birthday. They try to conceal their marital strife to avoid spoiling the occasion, but have to contend with the kids giving away all their secrets. Comedy drama, starring Rosamund Pike, David Tennant and Billy Connolly

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Angela Hartnett, and comedian Micky Flanagan. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Everton v Chelsea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to Llandudno to explore the rugged beauty of Great Orme, a giant headland overlooking the Irish Sea. Along the way he visits St Tudno's church and copper mines that date back 4,000 years. His next walk takes him to the remote Berwyn mountains, where he treks from Llangynog to the Pistyll Rhaeadr waterfall

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering the truth about football in Wales. It's a story of weekend warriors, boggy pitches and dodgy tackles, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair in tonight's episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie and Maisie perform a dance chosen by the judges, their showdance and their favourite routine from the series. The people with the paddles - Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse - will critique each performance, but it will be up to the viewers alone to determine who will be taking the glitterball from reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher. There'll also be a performance by pop superstar Robbie Williams

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize, guided by tonight's seven celebrities, who include Chris Kamara, Gyles Brandreth and Carol Vorderman

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present the latest action, including the Thursday night match between Las Vegas Raiders and Los Angeles Chargers

programme.title: Notes on a Scandal (2006)
programme.desc: Lonely history teacher Barbara Covett's acerbic personality means she has few friends. When a spirited woman takes a job in the art department of the school where Barbara works, the elderly lady develops a dark obsession with her new colleague, and the discovery of an affair with a student sets her on a mission to destroy the life of her new 'friend'. Drama, starring Judi Dench, Cate Blanchett, Bill Nighy, Tom Georgeson and Michael Maloney

programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special

programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul travels to the very north of the British Isles, to The Shetland Isles - islands which are as Scandinavian as they are British. During his northerly adventures, Paul lands on the island of Yell, where he meets with local Brydon Thomason, from Shetland Nature, and gets the amazing opportunity to see wild otters up close.

programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2020 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge.
