programme.desc: Al takes Jimmi, Emma and Valerie on a spooky Christmas outing where they end up confronting some of their own personal demons

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple hunting for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £380,000, and meets traditional dairy producers reviving a village's cheese-making heritage

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Witley Recycling Centre, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Ollie Allen helping to transform a chair, a wooden chest and a medieval-style tent

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon's search takes them to Devon, where items include a 1920s flapper dress, violins, trinket boxes and a walking stick with a maritime connection

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki ignites a war and Jack delivers some unwanted news. As Tina's trust in Gray wavers, she seeks out answers

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain continues her culinary journey with a visit to California, home to one of the most diverse populations in America. She is invited to a Mexican quinceanera - an extravagant fiesta celebrating a girl's 15th birthday - cooks for an all-woman daredevil rodeo riding team and makes handmade fortune cookies with a grandma in one of the last remaining workshops in San Francisco's Chinatown

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2020 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge. They are given three hours and 15 minutes to cook a starter, a main course and a dessert, each of which takes their culinary abilities to a whole new level, with flawless presentation and exciting flavours. Judges Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Gregg Wallace assess their efforts, before deciding the winner

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund and Lord Melchett are embarrassingly kidnapped by a maniacal German master of disguise with designs on the English throne. Unfortunately for them, the Queen decides to pay only one of the ransom demands. Comedy, guest starring Hugh Laurie, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Fortitude star Robert Sheehan appears as himself in this festive episode of the sitcom. The residents of the council estate where Conor and Jock live are threatened with eviction, but are not about to go down without a fight - and the resulting media circus puts everyone on edge. In the midst of all this mayhem, Conor's mother Mairead is rushed to hospital, and may be more seriously ill than previously thought. When she says she wants to see a white Christmas, the lads vow to make her wish come true - with a little help from Sheehan, who is shooting a film nearby

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Lichfield, Staffordshire, Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent and Sutton in Surrey

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: There's a huge boost to the breeding programme, as an anteater gives birth in the run-up to Christmas. As Kate Humble reviews the footage with the keepers it reveals some worrying behavior from the mum towards her newborn. their stable at night in the winter months. Meanwhile, Ben Fogle contemplates whether reindeer really fly and Jean Johansson gives the lions their Christmas stockings and continues to find out if any animals really like sprouts.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross trawls through the programme's archives to select the best gifts bought by the teams from Christmases past and shares some trade secrets on what to look out for when seeking great festive presents. Plus, Anita Manning learns about the first Christmas cards and visits celebrity cook Rosemary Shrager to try out some festive treats with a difference

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple want to spend their retirement in the peace and quiet of the Scottish borders and Alistair Appleton is on hand to help them find the perfect pad for their £350,000 budget. Alistair also visits a mill that has been weaving the country's iconic fabric, tweed, since the early 19th century

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne searches for three items he can rescue and revamp at the recycling centre in Woking. An old plans chest needs some careful reworking at the hands of restorers Tom and Johnny, while woodworking wizard Norman Wilkinson is challenged with creating high-end furniture from a set of discarded garden chairs. EJ himself attempts to repurpose a pair of swivel stools

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: This south-west adventure concludes with a tour of Devon, Somerset and Dorset for auctioneers Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon. Izzie visits a top secret location and makes a discovery, while Southon buys some Georgian shoe buckles and an anchor. Izzie also plans an upset at the final auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones introduces the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by cyclist Rachel Atherton, cricketer Ollie Pope, six-time world swimming champion Mark Foster and former footballer Clinton Morrison. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie reaches its peak as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda makes a decision from which there is no coming back. Kheerat tries to do the right thing and is surprised at what he finds. It's time to cast the role of Walford's bad Santa - and with Kim taking the reins, things quickly descend into chaos

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: For brothers Nathan and Jake Carter, Christmas means family, music and craic, and while this year might be a little different they are determined to celebrate the only way they know how - a good old country music-inspired celebration from their Fermanagh home. The musical duo perform a host of their favourite Christmas tunes and introduce some very special guests, including Lisa McHugh, Robert Mizzell and Cliona Hagan

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Comedy couple Marcus Brigstocke and Rachel Parris and local comedian Diona Doherty join regulars Neil Delamere and Colin Murphy as they raise a socially distanced laugh or two in response to current events. Hosted by Tim McGarry

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to George Clooney about his new sci-fi movie The Midnight Sky. Michael Sheen and David Tennant discuss playing locked-down actors in the second series of Staged, while Oscar-winner Viola Davis talks about the drama Ma Rainey's Black Bottom. Vanessa Kirby reflects on domestic drama Pieces of a Woman, Daisy May Cooper discusses her latest work, and Michael Ball and Alfie Boe perform Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs for a compilation that looks back at the big news of 2020

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Grandad has got it into his head that the house is haunted, Rory hopes to improve his looks by undergoing plastic surgery and Cathy continues her search for the right man by getting herself an internet date. As for Agnes, her annual battles with the decorations may well be over as Buster has bought her a special tree. What could possibly go wrong? Brendan O'Carroll stars as the daft-but-doting Irish matriarch

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The family members finish their big Christmas Day dinner and Barbara senses that her turkey wasn't up to scratch. Soon after Antony leaves for girlfriend Emma's house and Dave departs to take Nana home, Denise goes into labour upstairs in the bathroom and Jim tries to comfort her. Festive episode of the comedy from 1999

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Angela Hartnett, and comedian Micky Flanagan. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Everton v Chelsea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall looking for two things to save from the clutches of the tip crusher. With the help of artisan Norman Wilkinson, Sarah hopes to turn a profit

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair in tonight's episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Comedian Omid Djalili, reality star Charlotte Crosby, TV presenter Dr Zoe Williams and former England rugby international James Haskell answer questions on the Harry Potter Films, Curb Your Enthusiasm, Nelson Mandela, and Whales, Dolphins and Porpoises

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie and Maisie perform a dance chosen by the judges, their showdance and their favourite routine from the series. The people with the paddles - Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse - will critique each performance, but it will be up to the viewers alone to determine who will be taking the glitterball from reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher. There'll also be a performance by pop superstar Robbie Williams

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize, guided by tonight's seven celebrities, who include Chris Kamara, Gyles Brandreth and Carol Vorderman

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present the latest action, including the Thursday night match between Las Vegas Raiders and Los Angeles Chargers

programme.title: Notes on a Scandal (2006)
programme.desc: Lonely history teacher Barbara Covett's acerbic personality means she has few friends. When a spirited woman takes a job in the art department of the school where Barbara works, the elderly lady develops a dark obsession with her new colleague, and the discovery of an affair with a student sets her on a mission to destroy the life of her new 'friend'. Drama, starring Judi Dench, Cate Blanchett, Bill Nighy, Tom Georgeson and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: On the 70th anniversary of one of her career-defining films All about Eve, Talking Movies looks back at the life and times of Bette Davis, regarded as one of the greatest actresses in Hollywood history. Davis worked for more than 60 years and made over 100 films, including classics such as Whatever Happened to Baby Jane? and Dark Victory. She was a trailblazer for the independent actress who feuded with studio bosses at a time when women in Hollywood were not expected to be assertive. The programme includes of one of the last major interviews Davis gave on a film set to Talking Movies host Tom Brook, filmed on location in Maine where she was shooting her penultimate film, The Whales of August, which co-starred silent screen icon Lillian Gish. This tribute to Davis also includes interviews with younger actresses who still regard Davis as an inspirational force and an assessment of her body of work from a range of critics, film-makers, producers and her many fans. programme.title: 100 Women
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Inuit people lived off the land. Nomadic hunters, chasing targets depending on the seasons. Now those weather patterns have utterly and irreversibly shifted. Locals say that sea ice used to form in September. Now, it forms in October or as late as November. The Arctic is now warming at least twice as fast as the rest of the globe, according to the Arctic Program Report Card. Climate change is threatening the Inuits’ survival - both physically and mentally. Inuit women are trying to hold their families together through it all. Life on Thin Ice follows three generations of Inuit women; a grandmother, mother and daughter to see the impact that climate change has had on their community. What else have they lost along with the ice?

programme.title: 100 Women
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Inuit people lived off the land. Nomadic hunters, chasing targets depending on the seasons. Now those weather patterns have utterly and irreversibly shifted. Locals say that sea ice used to form in September. Now, it forms in October or as late as November. The Arctic is now warming at least twice as fast as the rest of the globe, according to the Arctic Program Report Card. Climate change is threatening the Inuits’ survival - both physically and mentally. Inuit women are trying to hold their families together through it all. Life on Thin Ice follows three generations of Inuit women; a grandmother, mother and daughter to see the impact that climate change has had on their community. What else have they lost along with the ice?

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Documentary following life in the glamorous London hotel over the festive season, as staff prepare to welcome guests during the hotel's most profitable time of the year. Head butler Sean braces new recruit Michael for how busy he is going to be, while restaurant director Anne prepares the four restaurants and her 160 staff. Cameras also follow regular guest Howell Harris as he spends his first Christmas at the hotel since his mother died, with staff going above and beyond to make sure he has a holiday to remember

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des O'Connor went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard

But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

After checking into their Hotel in the Bavarian Alps, Harry receives the German team sheet, leaving him concerned his team are going to have to massively step up their game. With the fear of the match going to penalties hanging over their head, Harry brings in a German psychologist to offer some advice. Finally the big day arrives, and Harry has managed to recruit Manchester United legend Teddy Sheringham, so the team finally has its missing striker

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John and Lisa offer up a super simple menu for ‘the big day' in their Christmas Kitchen, starting with a delicious breakfast - a cheesy croissant bake served with a cherry bellini. That is followed by John's crab cocktail, a Christmas dinner traybake, and Christmas pudding parcels and cream

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef enjoys a trip to Southfork, the ranch made famous in TV soap Dallas. While in town, James visits the location of the Texas State Fair and shares his own pastiche of the world-famous drama series

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Comedy, with Jim Carrey, Maura Tierney, Justin Cooper, Jennifer Tilly and Cary Elwes

programme.title: Celebrity Juice
programme.desc: Chart star Paloma Faith, presenter and friend of the show Paddy McGuinness, Kaiser Chiefs frontman Ricky Wilson and football pundit Chris Kamara join Keith Lemon, Laura Whitmore and Emily Atack for this festive special of the comedy quiz

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: As the Griffins prepare for the festive season by decorating their tree, Meg finds an ornament of the manger and Peter tells the story of Christmas - with a twist. His account begins with Joseph and his friend Robby spotting Mary in their village and becoming acquainted. After many dates, the future Holy guardian is annoyed by his girlfriend's reluctance to have sex, and she claims there is something special intended for her

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and her friends take a shine to hunky reporter Dallas, who looks set to replace Tom Tucker as news anchor, Brian pretends to be a millionaire to impress a new love interest

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris is named as homecoming king, but Meg learns that the cool kids plan to prank him during the ceremony, while Peter gets jealous when Cleveland starts hanging out with Jerome

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian falls for a tech support girl in India and sets out to find her, with Stewie in tow. Meanwhile, Peter steals the show at Joe's bingo night

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: In his continuing quest to meet girls, Steve decides to try to earn a letter jacket by joining the school's water polo team. When his initial attempt fails, he enlists Klaus' help to make the squad, and soon becomes the team's star player. Meanwhile, Stan and Roger buy a sailboat, but find life on water is not so easy as they imagined

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Hayley visits a hypnotherapy doctor, who is really Roger in another of his alter-egos, and ends up regressing back to her six-year-old self. Meanwhile, Steve and his friends buy a slow cooker and try to make the perfect pork

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland discovers Rallo has a natural gift for the sheep-riding sport of mutton bustin' - but Donna learns it is unsafe and orders him to stop competing

programme.title: Through the Keyhole
programme.desc: Keith Lemon hosts a festive rummage through the properties of three mystery stars. As the host uncovers clues to their identities, panellists Jonathan Ross, Lorraine Kelly and Jimmy Carr must use their powers of detection and the guidance of the studio audience to guess who the celebrity homeowners are

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin tells Natalie he needs time to think. Maureen tells Maud about her one-night stand with Curly, and Bill warns him to keep away from Maureen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie asks Kevin to stay with her for the weekend and not see Sally. Maureen is furious with Bill for punching Curly. Judy is alarmed to see Liam bruised and bandaged

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy orders the Dingles to tidy up ahead of Paddy's mum's arrival. Viv insists to Vic that she did not hit Kelly. Ned is furious about Helen Ackroyd's article regarding Jan

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah advises Tony not to give up on his marriage. Paddy apologises to the Dingles, but makes things worse with Mandy. Becky gives Tony an ultimatum

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Phil and Geoff keep watch on the illegal gambling club owned by Kenny Dekin (guest Ron Donachie), in the hope of finding evidence to close the establishment. Rob is concerned about his future with Carol as he anxiously awaits the results of his sergeant exams, while David is pleased with the outcome when he strikes out on his own

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's cousin visits the Abbott household, and the bemused family have a terrible time getting rid of him. Sid James stars

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Intrigue spreads among the residents of Aidensfield as Frances Elliot causes havoc with her car. An outraged Peggy decides to investigate the rumours about the newcomer and discovers she appears to be hiding something. Rob receives some good news in the post but is later forced into making a life-changing decision. Kerrie Taylor guests

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack advises Tony not to believe that Becky will go through with the divorce. Paddy's mum pushes him to get in touch with his ex. Jez and Sam have an altercation in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles arrive at the magistrates court for Sam's hearing. Jan is shocked to read the article about her in the Hotten Courier. Kim asks Seth to give her away at her wedding

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily chases Alec for the raffle money to give to the hospital. Gary tells Judy he thinks Liam is trying to blackmail him. Samantha agrees to model for Angie

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec is furious when Emily doubts his honesty. Gary is amazed when Judy confesses to giving Liam money. Natalie tells Kevin she cannot cope without him

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Feature-length festive special. It is Christmas 1846 and Victoria is uncharacteristically sad as she prepares to face her first Christmas without her beloved Baroness Lehzen, while Albert transforms the palace into a magical festive wonderland as he is gripped by an obsession with staging the perfect family Christmas. But tensions between the couple mount when the prince invites house guests to join the celebrations. Meanwhile, Skerrett inherits a large estate from an estranged uncle. Starring Jenna Coleman and Tom Hughes

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: Violet witnesses Chandrika going into John's room at night, and tells his mother what she saw. Margaret is devastated when she finds out and resolves to leave Delhi. John realises his only hope of persuading her to stay is to reveal the truth about his past and the baby's identity - even if it puts the child's safety in jeopardy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The apparent accidental death of a drug addict takes a surprising turn when the results of the post-mortem examination are revealed, and Jack finds himself confronting his own chequered past. Ewen Bremner guest-stars, with David Jason

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: As the police try to piece together the fatal sequence of events, both women come under the spotlight, but have they got the right one? As the investigation spirals, Leah realises she must come face to face with Rose one last time, but it may be too late. Final episode of the drama, starring Katherine Kelly and Molly Windsor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: The team celebrate a triumphant trip to Corfu. Back home, a Greek night is spiced up with a viewing of their holiday video

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of bikers wreaks havoc in Aidensfield, prompting the locals to demand a new bobby. When young recruit PC Joe Mason (Joe McFadden) arrives he is not quite what they expected, with his tactics for crime fighting making his reception a mixed one between both residents and fellow police officers

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak takes advantage of Marlon being in charge at the wine bar. The Cairns family load up the car ready to leave for Germany. Alan tries to talk Eric out of his depression

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is furious about the mess the Dingles have caused at the tearooms. Steve is relieved when Kim agrees to sign his contract. Scott tells Lyn he's not happy in the army

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz entertains Maureen and Deirdre at Singles' Night at the Warwick. Gary rows with Judy for giving Zoe their money. Angie makes a pass at Chris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen is alarmed when Fred keeps calling her. Deirdre is thrilled when Jon asks her out. Kevin is stunned when Natalie tells him she loves him

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Dad's Army (2016)
programme.desc: The officious captain of a seaside town's home guard platoon tries to maintain the reputation and morale of his troops as the Second World War draws to a close. He finds himself dealing with the hunt for a German spy as well as a female journalist sent to report on the exploits of the brave but bumbling soldiers. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Toby Jones, Bill Nighy and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: John is shocked to discover that Margaret has left Delhi, but before he can react there is worse news at home. The truth behind his brother Daniel's relationship with the beautiful servant Chanchal is exposed with the revelation that she is pregnant, and the brothers come to blows. However, before it can escalate further, Castillon bursts into the house to arrest John for stealing a precious diamond owned by the Emperor - who has betrayed John and who can save him?

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: John has spent several days and nights in his cell and has finally worked out who betrayed him. Daniel, Margaret and Chandrika desperately try to help release him, but only the Emperor can let him go free - and this will only happen when he is told a hard truth. Back home, the house is attacked and the women surround an empty cot, sobbing. Who has taken baby August, and why? Period drama set in 18th-century Delhi, starring Tom Bateman, Lesley Nicol, Leo Suter, Dakota Blue Richards and Pallavi Sharda

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Feature-length festive special. It is Christmas 1846 and Victoria is uncharacteristically sad as she prepares to face her first Christmas without her beloved Baroness Lehzen, while Albert transforms the palace into a magical festive wonderland as he is gripped by an obsession with staging the perfect family Christmas. But tensions between the couple mount when the prince invites house guests to join the celebrations. Meanwhile, Skerrett inherits a large estate from an estranged uncle. Starring Jenna Coleman and Tom Hughes

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Faye announces that she has quit her job, Debbie asks Gary to reconsider his position, and Todd goes snooping in Paul and Billy's flat

programme.title: Carry On Camping (1969)
programme.desc: Two men lure their unsuspecting girlfriends to a nudist camp, with hopes of having a wild old time - but the joke is on the men when it turns out they have ended up at a traditional family campsite. Bawdy comedy, starring Sid James, Bernard Bresslaw, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims and Barbara Windsor

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At a Christmas family get-together, a cracker riddle states two people will die before midnight on Boxing Day, and just a few hours later family matriarch Lydia meets an untimely end - giving Barnaby the perfect reason to escape a boring Christmas with his in-laws. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama starring Brenda Blethyn. The detective investigates a fire that rips through a holiday park on the Northumberland coast, destroying three cabins. A young woman's body is discovered and the resort's owner, Jim Viner, fears the victim is his sister Deena when he returns from a weekend away with his daughter Clare. With Jim's suspicions confirmed, a police report then reveals Clare's boyfriend Ryan and his friends had been partying near the scene hours before the blaze started, but where are they now, and where is Deena's best friend Sally? Co-starring Kenny Doughty, Cush Jumbo and Jon Morrison

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective discovers his favourite 1970s band are on the verge of a comeback - along with their singer Esme Ford, who has made a shocking reappearance from beyond the grave, having faked her own death 35 years earlier. But when an orphan is found dead on the other side of town, a forensic link leads Lewis and Hathaway to suspect the aged rockers are somehow involved. Joanna Lumley, David Hayman, Simon Callow and Helen Baxendale guest star alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Dad's Army (2016)
programme.desc: The officious captain of a seaside town's home guard platoon tries to maintain the reputation and morale of his troops as the Second World War draws to a close. He finds himself dealing with the hunt for a German spy as well as a female journalist sent to report on the exploits of the brave but bumbling soldiers. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Toby Jones, Bill Nighy and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: John is shocked to discover that Margaret has left Delhi, but before he can react there is worse news at home. The truth behind his brother Daniel's relationship with the beautiful servant Chanchal is exposed with the revelation that she is pregnant, and the brothers come to blows. However, before it can escalate further, Castillon bursts into the house to arrest John for stealing a precious diamond owned by the Emperor - who has betrayed John and who can save him?

programme.title: Beecham House
programme.desc: John has spent several days and nights in his cell and has finally worked out who betrayed him. Daniel, Margaret and Chandrika desperately try to help release him, but only the Emperor can let him go free - and this will only happen when he is told a hard truth. Back home, the house is attacked and the women surround an empty cot, sobbing. Who has taken baby August, and why? Period drama set in 18th-century Delhi, starring Tom Bateman, Lesley Nicol, Leo Suter, Dakota Blue Richards and Pallavi Sharda

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Feature-length festive special. It is Christmas 1846 and Victoria is uncharacteristically sad as she prepares to face her first Christmas without her beloved Baroness Lehzen, while Albert transforms the palace into a magical festive wonderland as he is gripped by an obsession with staging the perfect family Christmas. But tensions between the couple mount when the prince invites house guests to join the celebrations. Meanwhile, Skerrett inherits a large estate from an estranged uncle. Starring Jenna Coleman and Tom Hughes

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Faye announces that she has quit her job, Debbie asks Gary to reconsider his position, and Todd goes snooping in Paul and Billy's flat

programme.title: Carry On Camping (1969)
programme.desc: Two men lure their unsuspecting girlfriends to a nudist camp, with hopes of having a wild old time - but the joke is on the men when it turns out they have ended up at a traditional family campsite. Bawdy comedy, starring Sid James, Bernard Bresslaw, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims and Barbara Windsor

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At a Christmas family get-together, a cracker riddle states two people will die before midnight on Boxing Day, and just a few hours later family matriarch Lydia meets an untimely end - giving Barnaby the perfect reason to escape a boring Christmas with his in-laws. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama starring Brenda Blethyn. The detective investigates a fire that rips through a holiday park on the Northumberland coast, destroying three cabins. A young woman's body is discovered and the resort's owner, Jim Viner, fears the victim is his sister Deena when he returns from a weekend away with his daughter Clare. With Jim's suspicions confirmed, a police report then reveals Clare's boyfriend Ryan and his friends had been partying near the scene hours before the blaze started, but where are they now, and where is Deena's best friend Sally? Co-starring Kenny Doughty, Cush Jumbo and Jon Morrison

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective discovers his favourite 1970s band are on the verge of a comeback - along with their singer Esme Ford, who has made a shocking reappearance from beyond the grave, having faked her own death 35 years earlier. But when an orphan is found dead on the other side of town, a forensic link leads Lewis and Hathaway to suspect the aged rockers are somehow involved. Joanna Lumley, David Hayman, Simon Callow and Helen Baxendale guest star alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar plays his part in a gangster's plot to trick his four greatest enemies into carrying out a robbery. Stephanie Beacham features in an early role, alongside Roger Moore

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A top model is murdered by a hit-and-run driver. Dempsey and Makepeace discover she had connections at the highest level in government circles. When the two detectives make further investigations they uncover some disturbing facts.

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry protects a widowed garage owner from her grasping family and Arthur spots a chance to achieve one of his greatest ambitions - getting his hands on a Rolls-Royce. Lionel Jeffries and Keith Barron guest star alongside Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the death of a burglar who was killed after seeing classified information about a foreign country, and resort to theft in a bid to uncover the embassy's sinister secrets. Liz Fraser and Michael Elphick guest star

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar has to find out whether a cool blonde is a secret agent or a crook - and if she could do him harm. Sixties spy adventure, directed by and starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the afternoon session on day four from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Reigning champion Neil Robertson will hope to defend his title as he goes up against the top 32 money earners on the one-year list. This afternoon, the last 16 are in action, as the remaining players compete in best-of-seven-frame matches for a place in the quarter-finals. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to keep a surveillance on Cowley after evidence suggests he has stolen two canisters of a lethal nerve gas and begun negotiating with an unfriendly foreign power. The plot thickens when they trace their boss to a country mansion, where after they are caught he denies knowing them

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the evening session on day four from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Reigning champion Neil Robertson will hope to defend his title as he goes up against the top 32 money earners on the one-year list. Tonight, the first two quarter-finals take place, with players competing in best-of-nine-frame matches - tonight's winners will meet in tomorrow's semi-final. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

Sci-fi adventure, starring Taylor Kitsch, Liam Neeson and Alexander Skarsgard

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The resources of CI5 are stretched when a mentally unbalanced former soldier starts killing hospital staff, believing them responsible for the death of his wife and for the brain damage suffered by his young daughter. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A business tycoon is blackmailed for his part in a massacre while serving in the Army 25 years earlier. However, his problems are compounded after he seeks help from his former commanding officer. Colin Jeavons guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents action from Ascot on day one of the Christmas meeting at the Berkshire track. Today's feature races include the Kennel Gate Novices' Hurdle and the Noel Novices' Chase, both of which will see horses taking the next step on the road to Cheltenham. Analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and Adele Mulrennan, reporting from Oli Bell, Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey and Matt Chapman and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the double session on day five from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Reigning champion Neil Robertson will hope to defend his title as he goes up against the top 32 money earners on the one-year list. This afternoon, a double session of snooker begins with the remaining two quarter-finals and concludes with the first semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames. Robertson booked his place in the final in February with a 6-4 victory over Kyren Wilson, ending a fine run for Wilson that had seen him defeat 2019 title-winner Judd Trump and four-time world champion John HIggins. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Grade One Marsh Hurdle from Ascot, which is followed by races at 3.00 and 3.35. Plus, two races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Tommy Whittle Chase. With analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and AP McCoy, reports by Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey, Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Return of the Seven (1966)
programme.desc: The leader of a gang of bandits enslaves all the men from a Mexican village while maddened with grief over the death of his son. However, one of his hostages is a survivor from the original team of gunfighters, and his wife persuades his two remaining companions to form a new band of heroes and mount a rescue mission. Western sequel, starring Yul Brynner, Robert Fuller and Warren Oates

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the second semi-final on day six from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Neil Robertson and Graeme Dott both prevailed 6-4 against Kyren Wilson and Tom Ford respectively at this stage when the tournament was last held in February at Cheltenham, with Robertson going on to win the title. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Safe House (2012)
programme.desc: A CIA agent stationed in South Africa is sent to question a rogue operative, who has been arrested years after betraying the agency, only to be attacked by mercenaries hired to capture the fugitive. As they flee for their lives, the interrogator has to contend with not only the enemies hunting them, but also the mind games of his prisoner. Thriller, with Denzel Washington and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Return of the Seven (1966)

Western sequel, starring Yul Brynner, Robert Fuller and Warren Oates

programme.desc: Four-year-old pug and beagle cross Barney is brought into Fitzpatrick Referrals after being hit by a bus. The dog has lost 40 per cent of the bone in the damaged leg, so Noel creates an innovative two-tier treatment, involving an external ankle frame for support. Sisters Rosemary and Diane bring in their five-year-old basset hound Albert when they find he is suddenly unable to move his back legs, and 10-month-old Labrador cross Gylly receives treatment for lameness and an unusual bone deformity

programme.title: A Cure for Wellness (2016)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The young genius and Dr Sturgis fall out for the first time when Dr Sturgis marks a question as wrong on Sheldon's test paper. Meanwhile, Mary takes over Pastor Jeff's duties when he's off sick

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The youngster boycotts his favourite bread company when the taste of his favourite loaf changes, but matters soon get out of control. Meanwhile, Georgie tries to console Veronica after breaking up with her boyfriend

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The youngster becomes inspired by his hero Albert Einstein and tries to play the violin. Mary thinks that she may be pregnant and goes to buy a pregnancy test with Meemaw

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam tries to get a date with a psychiatrist who is a colleague of Frasier and Lilith, but she will not give him the time of day, so he makes an appointment to see her in her office

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woody's well-meant but modest birthday present fails to impress his wealthy girlfriend, while Rebecca decides a new look will improve her career prospects. Woody Harrelson stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A psychotherapist arrives to interview Debra for a book on the American family, but finds the rest of the household far more captivating, leaving the humble housewife feeling left out

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The Barones reminisce about more peaceful times in their first apartment and recall why they ended up living across the road from Ray's meddlesome parents

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Eddie appears depressed, so Martin enlists the aid of a dog psychiatrist to determine the root of his misery, much to Frasier and Niles's amusement. However, the diagnosis is that Eddie is picking up negative vibes from everyone around him, causing the Cranes and Daphne to assess their own lives. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A spontaneous gesture lands the Crane brothers a double date with two beautiful women, and Frasier tries to help Niles overcome the feeling that he is betraying Maris when they all take a trip to a cabin in the country. Comedy, guest starring Megan Mullally (Will & Grace) and Lisa Darr (Ellen), with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers need to impress judges Prue Leith and Liam Charles with their pastry-plaiting skills, in a challenge that offers the contenders of Heat A their last chance to go through to Finals week. Hosted by Harry Hill

programme.desc: In Dumfries and Galloway, Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole meet Alan, who works in the forestry business. Henry decides to give four bar stools a dayglo makeover, while Simon transforms two old sheep shearer seats into an extraordinary bench

programme.desc: Sarah from Wiltshire fell in love with Bulgaria 30 years ago when she was working as a teacher, and now she can't wait to buy a holiday home there. She loves the traditional town of Tryavna's authentic atmosphere and beautiful countryside, and is after a three-bedroom house for her budget of £40,000. Jonnie Irwin shows Sarah and her best friend Suzie five properties to consider

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Gloucestershire, hairdresser Lorna hopes an American '50s diner-style menu will be a success. A basic melon ball starter and a cremated American platter main don't seem to have the desired effect on her guests, and a disastrous dessert takes things from bad to worse

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge receives a jail sentence for child neglect after letting Bart go to the park unsupervised. While prison serves as a welcome break from her hectic home life, Homer struggles to hold the fort in her absence

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Strong men are left quaking by the news that alcohol has been made illegal in Springfield. Luckily salvation is at hand in the form of prohibition-busting bootlegger Homer, who brews his own beer to meet demand. Featuring the guest voice of Joe Mantegna

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: The UK has become the first country in the world to approve the coronavirus vaccine. But how have we managed to get a vaccine so quickly and just how safe is it? What do we know about possible long-term side effects and which vaccine is the safest and most effective to take? In a Dispatches special, Matt Frei looks at the vaccines and asks whether this is the shot in the arm to get Britain moving again

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George is joined by designer, craftsman and maker Will Hardie on trips to Norway, Finland, the Alps and Canada to check out some amazing winter designs - from ice hotels to stunning modern homes. Along the way, the duo build an igloo, race toboggans, feed reindeer and dodge a bear

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A chance to catch-up with some of the best moments from this year's episodes, featuring some of the country's favourite celebrities. The shows featured are This Morning, Planet Earth II, Big Brother: Best Shows Ever, Grease, Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back, World Cup Final '66, Dating Around and Misery

Regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley and lexicographer Susie Dent are on hand to adjudicate with guests Adam Buxton and Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: The Inbetweeners Movie (2011)
programme.desc: Awkward teenagers Simon, Will, Jay and Neil plan a holiday to the Greek island of Crete after they finish their exams, hoping to experience drink, sex and debauchery beyond their wildest dreams. However, things do not quite work out as planned. Comedy based on the TV series, with Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In the long-running animation's 26th Halloween special, Sideshow Bob finally kills Bart, only to reanimate him. Homer wakes up with short-term memory loss, and Lisa, Bart, and Milhouse get superpowers from radiation

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Glamorous Hollywood star Sara Sloane visits Springfield and becomes infatuated with Ned Flanders, who finds himself asking Homer for dating advice. Featuring the voices of Helen Fielding and Marisa Tomei

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is distressed to learn that Lenny and Carl fixed the result of a senior class presidential election, which he lost in high school - leading him to speculate on how his life would have changed for the better had he won the ballot

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart uses industrial adhesive to stick assorted novelty items to his face, while Lisa persuades Homer to let her visit the museum - but takes the wrong bus and becomes lost in an unfamiliar part of Springfield

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer discovers a talent for styling hair and opens a salon that becomes very popular, but begins to feel his gift is a curse as he must listen to people's problems and gossip. Meanwhile, Milhouse decides to live each day to the fullest and professes his love for Lisa - only to have his heart broken

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa gets her hands on a collection of keys to every door in Springfield, and soon uncovers a hidden classroom beneath the school, telling Principal Skinner about her discovery. He confiscates the key from her, but she remains determined to investigate the mysterious room

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Charity fundraiser Rachel Speight-McGregor kicks off a week of festive dinner parties in York. She hopes a full-on evening's entertainment, including carol singers on arrival, will impress her guests - baristar Harry Baines, pub landlady Estella Nicholson, administrative assistant Nicola Gregory and junior doctor Gyles Morrison - and see her on her way to securing the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Junior doctor Gyles Morrison serves a uniquely Caribbean take on a traditional Christmas dinner. Administrative assistant Nicola Gregory is captivated by his charm and keen to demonstrate her affection for the laid-back medic, but the other guests are less enamoured by their host as the dinner party challenge continues in York

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the third edition from York, vegetarian host Nicola Gregory hopes to convert four carnivores with her completely meat-free festive menu. However, the discovery by junior doctor Gyles Morrison and pub landlady Estella Nicholson of deer heads mounted in Nicola's garage leads the guests to question her vegan credentials. Meanwhile, charity fundraiser Rachel Speight-McGregor is more interested in drinking shots of salad dressing, and barista Harry Baines is concerned about his gift of a furry festive jock strap

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Harry Baines hosts the fourth festive edition of the competition from York and tries to prove to his fellow diners that his youth does not affect the quality of his cooking. To impress his guests he makes fiery cheesy focaccia, duck in blankets and mince pies

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pub landlady Estella Nicholson hosts the last Christmas dinner from York in her own premises, rustling up a menu including festive fishcakes, cheesy elf bakes, drunken lamb, veggie moussaka and sticky toffee pudding. Admin assistant Nicola Gregory is keen to make amends for offending another guest, and after an eventful week, the winner is decided

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp shows viewers some quirky ways to dress a dining table, and reveals how to make a paper napkin rose and a cupcake bouquet. The presenter also demonstrates a recipe for chocolate bowls

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart cheats during an exam and finds himself hailed as a child genius, but soon discovers he is not really cut out for the academic life - especially as it means being sent away to a special school

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart cheats during an exam and finds himself hailed as a child genius, but soon discovers he is not really cut out for the academic life - especially as it means being sent away to a special school

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer reluctantly pawns the TV to pay for family counselling sessions - but Dr Marvin Monroe's shocking treatment yields unexpected results

programme.title: Escape from Planet Earth (2013)
programme.desc: A heroic alien space explorer travels to Earth in response to a distress call. When he ends up being captured by the military and imprisoned in the infamous Area 51, it falls to his timid, nerdy brother to save him. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Rob Corddry, Brendan Fraser and Sarah Jessica Parker

programme.title: Heineken Champions Cup Rugby: The Big Tackle
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and guests present highlights and news from around the European rugby world, including a look ahead to this afternoon's game between Ulster and Gloucester

programme.title: The Bear
programme.desc: A large furry bear arrives from the zoo to take up temporary residence with a little girl, Tilly, and her parents. Children's animation based on the Raymond Briggs tale, with the vocal talents of Charlotte Church

programme.title: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
programme.desc: Animated adventure, based on the popular children's bedtime book by Michael Rosen and Helen Oxenbury. Stan, Katie, Rosie, Max and the baby, along with Rufus the dog, all embark on a quest to find some bears. Trekking through whirling snowstorms, muddy bogs and dark forests, the siblings refuse to let anything get in their way. However, when Rosie and Rufus end up getting lost, bear-hunting suddenly doesn't seem quite so much fun after all. Featuring the voices of Olivia Colman, Pam Ferris and Mark Williams as Mum, Grandma and Dad respectively

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill hosts as the next 10 contestants enter the tent to face a technical challenge involving multi-coloured batter, and then have to bake cakes to represent their families

programme.title: World Chase Tag - USA
programme.desc: Highlights from Atlanta, Georgia, as 16 teams from around America competed for the title in a knockout version of the playground game

programme.title: Live Heineken Champions Cup Rugby Union
programme.desc: Leinster v Northampton Saints (Kick-off 1.00pm). Lee McKenzie presents coverage of the match in the second round of Pool A fixtures, which takes place at RDS Arena in Dublin. Leinster are on the hunt for a record fifth title and the Saints are looking to avenge a defeat in the 2011 final, when Leinster came back from 22-6 down at half time to snatch the cup out of their hands with a 33-22 triumph. With analysis from Nolli Waterman, reports by Ed Jackson and commentary from Miles Harrison

programme.title: Jamie's Easy Christmas Countdown
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers his stress-free guide to planning the most important feast of the year, by showing exactly what to cook and when across Christmas Eve and Christmas Day. At the centre of his table is a show-stopping turkey with herb butter and crispy skin. That's surrounded by smoked salmon pate, pork stuffing crammed with silky leeks, sweet peaches and roasted chestnuts, glazed clementine carrots and gnarly sausage and sage

programme.title: Christmas at Chatsworth House
programme.desc: One-off documentary going behind the scenes of Chatsworth, one of England's most impressive stately homes, as the Duke, Duchess and their 800 staff brace themselves for an influx of 200,000 visitors over the 2019 festive season. Christmas is the most important event for the Derbyshire estate and crucial to its survival. In just five days, the house is transformed with 30 Christmas trees, 60,000 baubles, and a million fairy lights. But this year Derbyshire has been crippled by unprecedented flooding. Can family and staff rally to make this a season to be remembered?

programme.title: Jungle Mystery: Lost Kingdoms of the Amazon
programme.desc: Ella Al-Shamahi joins a complex police operation deep in the Amazon, as the authorities attempt to clamp down on illegal mining, which is poisoning the ecosystem of the rainforest. When the Spanish arrived in the Amazon 500 years ago, indigenous populations were devastated by disease and enslavement. Today's descendants of the Amazon's once flourishing civilisation are facing the same threats as a hostile world closes around them. Ella joins indigenous groups who are trying to fight back, formally marking land borders and using mobile phone technology to listen for illegal logging in the forest

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Barenboim on Beethoven
programme.desc: A remarkable insight into the mind and music of Ludwig van Beethoven, featuring highlights from Daniel Barenboim's 1970 series of programmes about the man and his music. Shot at London's Roundhouse Theatre, this compilation cherry picks some of the best moments, as Barenboim talks and plays extracts at the piano, illustrating the defining characteristics of Beethoven's compositions and sharing his personal insight into the composer's mindset. The full series of 13 episodes in their entirety - over seven hours, from the Fourth Piano Concerto to the Eroica to Beethoven's Fifth - follows on More4

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Manchester to explore the spirit of protest and revolution that grew out of the experience of Britain's working class during the Industrial Revolution. At the John Ryland Library, Alice is granted access to rarely-seen period documents that highlight the true human cost of the Peterloo massacre. At the Free Trade Hall, she explores how Manchester found itself at the heart of the battle against the hated Corn Laws, and at Chetham's Library, Roberts sits at the very desk where Friedrich Engels and Karl Marx, inspired by the living conditions of Manchester's working poor, began the discussions and research that would lead to the publication of the Communist Manifesto

programme.title: Forrest Gump (1994)
programme.desc: A slow-witted man has a series of bizarre adventures, including becoming an American football star, Vietnam veteran, table tennis champion and millionaire businessman - but the love of his life from childhood continues to elude him. Robert Zemeckis's Oscar-winning comedy drama, starring Tom Hanks, Sally Field, Robin Wright and Gary Sinise

programme.title: Carrie (1976)
programme.desc: A shy, withdrawn teenager discovers she has paranormal powers, which she uses to protect herself from her abusive mother and classmates. When a friendly student invites her to the prom, she is determined to enjoy herself and is amazed when she is crowned queen of the ball - but joy turns to fury when her tormentors play a cruel prank. Brian De Palma's adaptation of Stephen King's horror novel, with Sissy Spacek, Piper Laurie and Amy Irving

programme.title: Secrets of Your Supermarket Food
programme.desc: British shoppers buy 95 per cent of their food from just 10 supermarket chains. But how much do they really know about how this produce is grown, made and sold? Sian Williams and Stefan Gates present this investigative documentary in which they lift the lid on the secrets that the supermarkets do not want their customers to know

Ex-nurse Susie and husband Reg spend Christmas Day volunteering at a dog shelter in Estepona, and Doncaster lass Margaret belts out a few tunes with the choir - but they are a bit out of tune. programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at Hull Royal Infirmary to show the night-time challenges facing NHS staff working in the A&E department. Handcuffed and accompanied by four police officers, his behaviour has medical staff fearing for the wellbeing of other patients. Matron Kat Henry deals with a flirtatious patient who has cut his head after a night out on the town and is determined to leave the hospital without treatment

programme.title: Extraordinary People

programme.title: Inside the Steam Train Museum
programme.desc: Cameras capture seasonal events at Bressingham Steam Museum. Staff member Phil struggles when driving a vintage fire engine covered in Christmas lights to the local market town's Christmas event. Chris and the 'track team' replace a long stretch of railway line in the woods. Manager Al has to take over the role of Father Christmas in the grotto and Brian cooks a roast chicken in a steam engine to help celebrate the end of the year

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell follows traces the course of the Great Central Railway, the final great line of the Victorian era and the last main line built before the Channel Tunnel rail link more than a century later. Starting near Nottingham, Rob is taken aback by the scale of demolition and excavation needed to build this line through the city. Around Loughborough, Rob catches up with the major project that is now rebuilding bridges and 500 yards of track in order to link two heritage lines and restore a 20-mile section of the old route. He also visits Leicester Central station - once derelict but now set for a new life as a bowling alley

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer recalls two festive cruises in an edition featuring previously unseen footage. She starts on the SS Antoinette, travelling up the Rhine, then visits some of the world's best Christmas markets sampling traditional food and drink and getting to grips with unique customs. The Wakefield wonder then goes in search for a Nordic Crimbo on board the Norwegian institution the Coastal Express. Starting in Bergen, Jane feasts her eyes on the world's largest Gingerbread City then continues her cruise to visit Scandinavia's biggest natural aquarium for an encounter with a swimming Santa

programme.title: Live Boxing
programme.desc: Shakan Pitters v Craig Richards. All the action from the bout for the British Light Heavyweight title at Fly By Nite Rehearsal Studios, Redditch. Pitters recaptured the vacated belt and made it 14 fights undefeated as a professional in August with a wide points victory over Chad Sugden. Richards drew with Sugden when they met 12 months ago, but only has one loss on his record, which came at the hands of the more experienced Frank Buglioni in 2017

programme.title: Secret World of Las Vegas: 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary meeting some of the characters and hearing the stories that make the gambling capital of Las Vegas one of the most fun-filled cities on Earth
