programme.desc: Kate gets zapped by a scientist's shrinking ray, and ends up so small that Kim and Kylie cannot find her

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls take part in a fashion show, but soon discover that someone intends to sabotage the event

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zoo Babies
programme.desc: Documentary that follows the arrival of new members of the animal kingdom in zoos around the world, as well as showcasing endangered species being born in captivity

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: Booker T & the MGs guitarist Steve Cropper, who has worked with artists including Wilson Pickett, Otis Redding and Sam & Dave, talks to Ronnie about his lengthy and colourful career. The pair also discuss the records they both admire

programme.title: The Coronation of Queen Elizabeth II
programme.desc: Documentary charting the historic events of June 2, 1953, when the Queen became the 39th sovereign to be crowned at Westminster Abbey. What happened behind the scenes is explored through diaries, letters and government papers, as well as rare film footage and photographs, and there are also interviews with historians and experts on royal ceremonies. The programme reveals that Her Majesty's apparent calmness during the Coronation contrasted with organisers' last-minute nerves as the BBC mounted its most ambitious television broadcast to date, amid the concerns of prime minister Winston Churchill

programme.title: Daddy Daycare
programme.desc: Traditionally minded new father PJ hopes his skills as a management consultant will serve him well when he is sent to spend a week working at Tadpoles Nursery in Chelsea, west London, but his idea of arranging a football lesson for the youngsters ends in chaos. Meanwhile, gay budding parent Richard manages to lose track of two of his charges in an art gallery, and 21-year-old first-time dad Kadmiel gets off to a shaky start when placed in charge of snack time and reading practice for the nursery's four-year-olds

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Piccadilly Incident (1946)
programme.desc: A Wren is pronounced missing in action, but returns three years later to find her husband has remarried. Melodrama, starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding

programme.title: Miracle in Soho (1957)
programme.desc: A cynical road-repairer falls for the alluring charms of an Italian girl working as a barmaid, only to discover she and her family are planning to move to Canada. Romantic drama written by Emeric Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Belinda Lee and Cyril Cusack

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: Producer Mark Ronson compares notes with Ronnie about their varied approaches to the recording process, and the pair also discuss artists including Amy Winehouse and Stevie Wonder

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike is having financial trouble, so Laura decides to help out. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike's van and where he can park it interferes in the development of his relationship with Laura. Judi Dench and Michael Williams star

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Every day people make thousands of choices and behind all of them is the mind pitting intuition against logic. Psychologist and Nobel Prize-winner Daniel Kahneman explores this conflict that affects every aspect of life from diet to what money is spent on, revealing that the intuitive part of the brain can be surprisingly powerful

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The team offers advice to a 19-year-old fitness instructor who claims to have never eaten a vegetable, and a woman whose menopausal hot flushes are ruining her life. Also examined is a 39-year-old photographer whose diet may be causing her spine to fuse. The results of the first Big Food Trial are announced, and Pixie McKenna investigates the reliability of do-it-yourself food-intolerance tests

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom discovers he would qualify for a lump sum from his pension if he were married, so tries to strike up a deal with Sally. Meanwhile, Amber decides to impress her new boss by taking up cycling and, much to Tom's annoyance, receives tuition from Roy on how to ride a bike. Comedy, starring Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Jane Asher

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Triggermen (2002)
programme.desc: Two small-time British con-men are mistaken for professional killers and hired to assassinate a Chicago crime boss - a situation they are quick to exploit. Comedy, starring Donnie Wahlberg, Michael Rapaport and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: Love, Honour and Obey (1999)
programme.desc: A humble courier manages to wheedle his way into the confidence of north London's toughest mobsters. Disappointed by the lack of action, he sets out to make things more interesting - but inadvertently sparks a bloody gangland conflict. Comedy thriller, starring Jude Law, Sadie Frost, Jonny Lee Miller, Ray Winstone, Rhys Ifans, Kathy Burke and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: True Blue Confessions
programme.desc: A tribute to Mary Millington, Britain's number one sex symbol of the 1970s

programme.title: A Chorus of Disapproval (1988)
programme.desc: A shy widower gets entangled in romantic intrigue while rehearsing with an amateur theatrical troupe in Scarborough. Comedy based on Alan Ayckbourn's stage play, starring Jeremy Irons, Anthony Hopkins, Prunella Scales, Lionel Jeffries, Jenny Seagrove, Gareth Hunt and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike and Laura agree to go on a date, but chaos ensues in the restaurant when Laura loses a contact lens. Starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Laura buys a new dress for a special dinner with Ben. However, when her date reveals he is married, Mike happens by to pick up the pieces with his usual charm. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The crew seek the help of a druid in curing Faxe's stomach ache, but he steals their ship

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar plans to steal all the treasure on Dream Island, but the enchantress who lives there puts the crew to sleep

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: Kim is brainwashed into liking hip hop, and the other girls are forced to change their music to accommodate her new obsession

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls take on the Mixed Tapes on a game show, but their rivals cheat by exploiting their worst fears

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zoo Babies
programme.desc: Documentary that follows the arrival of new members of the animal kingdom in zoos around the world, as well as showcasing endangered species being born in captivity

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The guitarist talks to his current Faces bandmate Mick Hucknall. The duo perform a version of Bob Dylan's One of Us Must Know, and Ronnie shares his memories of Jimi Hendrix

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The team offers advice to a 19-year-old fitness instructor who claims to have never eaten a vegetable, and a woman whose menopausal hot flushes are ruining her life. Also examined is a 39-year-old photographer whose diet may be causing her spine to fuse. The results of the first Big Food Trial are announced, and Pixie McKenna investigates the reliability of do-it-yourself food-intolerance tests

programme.title: City Swimmers
programme.desc: For hundreds of years, English men and women have braved arctic temperatures to swim in London's murky ponds. When threatened with closure by the Council, a campaign was launched to save them

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Blitz Spirit (2019)
programme.desc: Short film set during the London Blitz, in which a pianist struggles to justify his war efforts to his stubborn mother

programme.title: Doctor in the House (1954)
programme.desc: A medical student is taken under the wing of three roguish colleagues who lead him astray into a series of pranks and amorous escapades that succeed in infuriating the dictatorial chief surgeon. Comedy based on the novel by Richard Gordon, starring Dirk Bogarde, Kenneth More, James Robertson Justice and Donald Sinden

programme.title: We Dive at Dawn (1943)
programme.desc: A British submarine crew is unexpectedly ordered to carry out a top-secret mission to sink the German fleet's latest battleship in the Baltic Sea. But it is not long before disaster strikes and the vessel becomes stranded in the middle of the ocean with no fuel or food. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Bankside and East Dulwich for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist and former Faces bandmate Ian McLagan reminisce about their time on the road together in the 1970s, and discuss the band's 2011 reunion tour

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen seeks a boyfriend for her socially inept elder sister Laura, so invites nervous horticulturalist Mike to a party. The pair discover they share a dislike of being fixed up, so depart together to prevent Helen objecting to them leaving. Bob Larbey's comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen and Phil are keen to know how things went between unlikely lovebirds Laura and Mike, so both end up invited to lunch - where they relate conflicting stories. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom discovers he would qualify for a lump sum from his pension if he were married, so tries to strike up a deal with Sally. Meanwhile, Amber decides to impress her new boss by taking up cycling and, much to Tom's annoyance, receives tuition from Roy on how to ride a bike. Comedy, starring Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Jane Asher

programme.title: Great SAS Missions
programme.desc: Computer graphics, reconstructions and interviews help to profile the covert operation to seek and destroy Hitler's V-2 rockets. The Vergeltungswaffe 2 was the world's first ballistic missile - and, with a range of 186 miles, could be fired from the continent to strike London

programme.title: Eat to Live Forever with Giles Coren
programme.desc: The food critic takes up three extreme regimens in an effort to push the limits of life expectancy and meets people from around the world whose pursuit of longevity is an obsession that dominates every aspect of their lives. He undertakes the calorie restriction diet, before going on one that consists only of foods hunted, fished or gathered by mankind's Paleolithic ancestors and ends with another made up almost entirely of fruit. Throughout the process, Giles's health is monitored by his doctor, who assesses the impact these dietary habits are having on his body

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s, telling the history of folk and protest music. Featuring interviews and footage of artists including Joan Baez, Woody Guthrie and Leonard Cohen

programme.title: Love, Honour and Obey (1999)
programme.desc: A humble courier manages to wheedle his way into the confidence of north London's toughest mobsters. Disappointed by the lack of action, he sets out to make things more interesting - but inadvertently sparks a bloody gangland conflict. Comedy thriller, starring Jude Law, Sadie Frost, Jonny Lee Miller, Ray Winstone, Rhys Ifans, Kathy Burke and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: The Legend of Barney Thomson (2015)
programme.desc: A mild-mannered Glasgow barber finds his mundane life turned upside down when he accidentally commits murder. Soon one killing leads to another, and his desperate efforts to cover up his crimes leads him to uncover his mother's dark secrets. Black comedy based on the series of books by Douglas Lindsay, directed by and starring Robert Carlyle, with Emma Thompson and Ray Winstone

programme.title: Deceitful Love (2016)
programme.desc: The lives of a wealthy British Indian family are turned upside down when dark family secrets are made public after a family bereavement. Drama, starring Indiraa and Gurdial Sira

programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s, telling the history of folk and protest music. Featuring interviews and footage of artists including Joan Baez, Woody Guthrie and Leonard Cohen

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Buttons: A Christmas Tale (2018)
programme.desc: An orphan facing the propspect of life on the street receives a visit from a guardian angel, who comforts her with the story of another child's search for a family and the role another mysterious helper played in her life. Children's fantasy, starring Angela Lansbury and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Lady with the Lamp (1951)
programme.desc: Biopic of Florence Nightingale, who went down in medical history for her heroic efforts to treat thousands of casualties during the Crimean War, while the British Government belittled her for being a woman. Starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding

programme.title: Time Without Pity (1957)
programme.desc: An alcoholic writer returns home after years living abroad when he learns his estranged son has been convicted of murder and sentenced to hang. Although his attempts at reconciliation are rejected, he spends the last 24 hours before the execution trying to prove his son is innocent by identifying the real killer. Mystery, starring Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern and Peter Cushing

programme.title: Oxford Street
programme.desc: A Montage of the famous street around Christmas time in 1970, shot on 16mm film

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: The team offers advice to a 19-year-old fitness instructor who claims to have never eaten a vegetable, and a woman whose menopausal hot flushes are ruining her life. programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien may have ulterior motives for inviting Tracey to a posh event, while Sharon makes a tidy profit out of fake Rolling Stones T-shirts. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey spreads her wings for the first time since her husband was put behind bars, and invites a handsome footballer round for dinner. Meanwhile, Sharon discovers the joys of literature. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Four children with a taste for adventure investigate a strange mystery in between lashings of ginger beer during a trip to the country. Spoof of Enid Blyton's Famous Five books that first launched the comedy series, starring Peter Richardson, Adrian Edmondson, Jennifer Saunders and Dawn French

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Exception (2016)
programme.desc: A German soldier tries to determine if the Dutch resistance has planted a spy to infiltrate the home of Kaiser Wilhelm in Holland during the onset of the Second World War, but falls for a young Jewish-Dutch woman during his investigation. Drama, starring Lily James, Jai Courtney and Christopher Plummer

programme.title: The C Word
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith stars in this drama based on the blog and book by Lisa Lynch, an account of her life after being diagnosed with breast cancer. Twenty-eight-year-old Lisa's life is going exactly the way she wanted - she has recently married the man of her dreams and her career is on the up. Then out of the blue, she receives a shocking a diagnosis from the doctor that threatens to stop her in her tracks. Right from the start, however, Lisa is determined not to let cancer change her, and begins to write about her experience - unaware of the effect it will have on her life and that of others. Paul Nicholls, Haydn Gwynne and Michael Maloney co-star

programme.title: Triggermen (2002)
programme.desc: Two small-time British con-men are mistaken for professional killers and hired to assassinate a Chicago crime boss - a situation they are quick to exploit. Comedy, starring Donnie Wahlberg, Michael Rapaport and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: Booker T & the MGs guitarist Steve Cropper, who has worked with artists including Wilson Pickett, Otis Redding and Sam & Dave, talks to Ronnie about his lengthy and colourful career. The pair also discuss the records they both admire

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Live World Darts Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the concluding session of day three at Alexandra Palace in London, featuring three matches from the first round and one from the second round. Scot Peter Wright won this tournament for the first time last year, beating Michael van Gerwen 7-3 in the final. Wright became only the second Scottish player to win the competition after Gary Anderson, who won the tournament twice in 2015 and 2016.

programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: A look back at New England Patriots' 2003 Super Bowl victory over Carolina Panthers at the Reliant Stadium in Houston, Texas. The Patriots claimed a narrow 32-29 victory, in what is widely considered by pundits as one of the greatest Super Bowls of all time

programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: The story of Baltimore Ravens' 2000 Super Bowl success over New York Giants at the Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida. The Ravens became the second team in four years to win the Super Bowl after qualifying for the play-offs as one of the wildcard teams, and the 34-7 victory was the first for the franchise since leaving Cleveland under a huge cloud of controversy
